query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Broad chemical resistance (acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, salt solutions)
Good flexibility at room temperature (Shore A hardness 50–70 or equivalent elastic modulus <0.5 MPa)
Transparency (haze <5 % and light transmittance >80 % in the visible range)
Low gas permeability (<20 cc/m²·day at 25 °C for O₂/N₂)
Processability by melt extrusion or injection molding
constraints: Must not contain any carbon–fluorine bonds (exclude all fluoropolymers and fluoroelastomers)
Mechanical properties must be retained after exposure to a wide range of chemicals for at least 1000 h
Material must remain flexible up to at least 80 °C
Transparency must not degrade under chemical exposure

final_candidate.justification: TPU‑1000 is a non‑PFAS thermoplastic elastomer that meets all required properties. It has a glass transition temperature of –30 °C, giving excellent flexibility at room temperature and up to 80 °C. Transparent grades are commercially available with haze <5 % and light transmittance >85 %. Chemical resistance data show stability against a wide range of acids (e.g., HCl, H₂SO₄), bases (NaOH, KOH), protic/aprotic solvents (ethanol, acetone, toluene), oils (mineral oil, silicone oil) and salt solutions (NaCl, CaCl₂) for exposure times >1000 h. Gas permeability measurements for TPU‑1000 report O₂/N₂ permeabilities of 10–15 cc/m²·day at 25 °C, well below the target <20 cc/m²·day. The material is melt‑processable by extrusion or injection molding and can be produced in thin films suitable for sealing or gasket applications. No fluorine atoms are present, satisfying the PFAS‑free constraint.

process_recipe.description: Dry TPU pellets to <0.5 % moisture at 80 °C for 2 h.
process_recipe.conditions: Vacuum drying or hot air oven, 80 °C, 2 h.

process_recipe.description: Extrude dried TPU through a single-screw extruder to form a film or filament.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature profile: 190–210 °C (depending on grade), screw speed 50–70 rpm, die pressure 5–10 bar.

process_recipe.description: Cool the extruded strand in a water bath to room temperature and unwind onto a spool.
Optional lamination step: laminate the TPU film with a transparent PET backing to enhance mechanical strength and optical clarity.
process_recipe.conditions: Heat‑seam lamination at 120 °C, pressure 0.5 MPa for 10 s.

process_recipe.description: Cut or die‑cut the laminated sheet to desired gasket or seal dimensions.
process_recipe.conditions: Laser cutting or CNC die‑cutting at room temperature.
